bio.education: Greater Atlanta Christian School (GACS) University of Central Florida (UCF)
bio.biography:
Quick Facts
  • Daughter of Doug and Agnes, and sister to Alyssa
  • Has represented the United States in over 100 international games and served as captain for the U.S. Women’s Beach Handball National Team since 2018
  • Discovered team handball after seeing the sport on television during the 2012 London Olympics
  • At UCF, was a four-year letterwoman in softball and won two conference championships
  • Received the 2008 Dr. Richard E. Lapchick Award (for Athletic, Academic, and Community Excellence), 2008 UCF Iron Athlete Award, and 2008 Founder's Scholar Award (Top 1% in graduating class)
  • At GACS, was a four-sport high school varsity letter winner in softball, basketball, soccer, & track
  • Hobbies include any sand-based sport, coffee, farmer’s markets, stand-up paddle boarding, game nights/shows (was featured on the Price is Right), and being a committed door holder at Passion City Church.
Career Highlights
  • 2019 ANOC World Beach Games - 10th, Captain (Doha, Qatar)
  • 2019 North American & Caribbean (NORCA) Beach Handball Championships - 1st, Captain (Trinidad & Tobago)
  • 2018 Pan American Beach Handball Championships - 6th (Oceanside, California)
  • 2018 USATH Open National Championships (Indoor) - 1st (Myrtle Beach, SC)
  • 2017 USATH Open National Championships (Indoor) - 1st (Myrtle Beach, SC)
  • 2017 Busan International Competition (Indoor) - 4th (Busan, South Korea)
  • 2017 Pan American Championships (Indoor) - 5th (Buenos Aires, Argentina)
  • 2017 Pan American Championship NORCA Qualifier (Indoor) - 2nd (San Juan, Puerto Rico)
  • 2015 Pan American Championships (Indoor) - 10th (Havana, Cuba)
  • 2015 Pan American Championship NORCA Qualifier (Indoor) - 3rd, Top 10 Scorer (San Juan, Puerto Rico)
  • 2015 Pan American Games Qualifier (Indoor) - 2nd (Auburn, AL & Uruguay)
  • 2015 American Open Beach Handball Championships - 1st (Gulf Shores, AL)
  • 2014 American Open Beach Handball Championships - 1st (Gulf Shores, AL)
  • 2013 Pan American Championship (Indoor) - 8th (Santo Domingo, Dominican Republic)
  • 2013 USATH Open National Championships (Indoor) - 1st (Reno, Nevada)
  • 2013: Named to the U.S. Women's Senior National Team
World Championship Experience
  • 2018 IHF Beach Handball World Championships - 14th, Captain (Kazan, Russia)

Career Highlights

Quick Facts
  • A multi-sport standout for the Houston High School Hilltoppers, Vance was injured during a football game on April 1, 2009. Fifteen days later, his leg was amputated above the knee. With help from a Jackson, Miss. orthotics and prosthetics outfit, Vance learend to run again
  • After crushing the competition at the 2010 Endeavor Games in Edmond, Okla., Vance qualified for the U.S. Paralympics Track and Field Resident Team and moved to Chula Vista, Calif
  • Trains under the tutelage of sprints coach Joaquim Cruz, the 1984 800-meter Olympic gold medalist from Brazil.
Paralympic Experience

    • Paralympic Games Rio de Janeiro 2016, 4th (200m T42 - Men), 13th (100m T42 - Men)
    • Paralympic Games London 2012, silver (200m T42 - Men), 6th (Shot Put F42/44 - Men), 8th (100m T42 - Men)

  • Top finish: 5th – 2013 (100m T42 - Men), 2013 (Shot Put F42 - Men)

Quick Facts
  • Daughter of Michael O’Shea and Tauna Vandeweghe
  • First name is Colleen, but prefers to go by CoCo
  • Has one older brother, Beau
  • Mother, Tauna, represented Team USA at the Olympic Games in swimming (1976) and volleyball (1984)
  • Grandfather, Ernie, played for the New York Knicks
  • Is the granddaughter of 1952 Miss America Colleen Kay Hutchins
  • Started playing tennis with her brother at age 11
  • Tennis was the last sport she played in her early years before turning pro in April 2008
  • Is a self-described “California girl" and spends most of her off-season at the beach with her family.

Most Notable

  • Selected most valuable player and best outside spiker at 2015 Pan American Cup, her first international tournament
  • Led Team USA to gold at 2015 Pan Am Cup with a tournament-leading 50.4 kill percent to go with 81 total points
  • Started two matches during 2015 FIVB World Grand Prix preliminary round, including a team-best 20 points vs. Belgium on July 5
  • Led Team USA in scoring in five of six 2015 Pan American Games matches as the Americans won the title. Averaged 4.14 points per set in the Pan Am Games
  • Two-time Honda Award Winner for best collegiate volleyball player (2013-14, 2014-15)
  • AVCA Division I National Player of the Year for 2013

Major Competitions
2015: Pan American Cup (Gold)... FIVB World Grand Prix Preliminary Round... Pan American Games (Gold)...NORCECA Championship (Gold)

  • Pac 12 Player of the Year and Scholar-Athlete of the Year in 2014
  • Capital One Academic All-American of the Year in 2014
  • ESPNW National Player of the Year in 2014
  • Selected Gatorade National High School Player of the Year in 2010
  • Selected to U.S. Girls' Youth National Training Team in 2008
  • Consensus number-one high school recruit in the nation among the class of 2011
  • Honored at 2011 ESPY Awards
  • Graced cover of Volleyball Magazine's April 2011 issue
  • Elected Homecoming Queen her senior year in high school where she led Redlands East Valley High School to three CIF championship titles
  • Enjoys going to the beach and spending time with friends
  • Hobbies are watching Netflix, going to the beach, shopping, hanging out with friends
  • Mother, Tricia, played basketball at Loyola Marymount University from 1987-1991
  • Graduated from University of Washington in 2015 with a bachelor's in sociology
  • Started playing indoor volleyball in 2001 with Rancho Valley 12-1 in Rancho Cucamonga, Calif.

bio.fun_fact: Aspires to be an athletic trainer or physical therapist after completing his education and snowboarding career
bio.biography:
Quick Facts
  • Specialty: Men's Snowboardcross
  • Son of Rob and Michelle Vedder
  • Has one sibling, Mitchel
  • Hobbies include mountain biking, kite surfing, wakesurfing and skateboarding
  • Finished as the top junior snowboarder on the Nor-Am Cup circuit in 2014-15
  • Aspires to be an athletic trainer or physical therapist after completing his education and snowboarding career
Biographical Information

Jake Vedder began snowboarding at age 5 at Mt. Brighton, a local hill with just 250 feet of vertical drop—a stark contrast to the massive mountains where world cup competitions take place. What started as winter recreation quickly evolved into a passion that would carry him to international success.

Vedder's breakout year came in 2016 when he captured gold for the United States at the Youth Olympic Winter Games Lillehammer 2016, earned multiple NorAm Cup podiums and made his first world cup appearance. That Youth Olympic gold medal opened doors, earning him an invitation to train with the U.S. Snowboard Cross Development Team. By 2018, he had advanced to the national team's A squad and claimed a Junior World Championship gold medal in Cardrona, New Zealand. His steady progression continued as he accumulated six individual world cup podiums and one team podium between 2018 and 2024, establishing himself as a consistent performer on the international circuit.

Vedder earned his first Olympic nomination for the Olympic Winter Games Beijing 2022, where he delivered an impressive sixth-place finish in men's snowboard cross—the top result among all American competitors. He also competed in the mixed team event alongside teammate Faye Gulini, placing ninth. His Olympic debut marked a significant milestone in a career built on determination and creative training solutions. Living in Michigan, far from major snowboarding destinations, Vedder and his father designed and built a custom start gate system in their garage, complete with artificial snow and timing technology that tracks reaction times and speeds. This innovation has given Vedder a unique training advantage and reflects the resourcefulness that has defined his journey.

Beyond competition, Vedder remains deeply connected to his Michigan roots and committed to giving back to his community. He regularly visits children's hospitals throughout metro Detroit, spending time with young patients and sharing his Olympic experiences. His partnership with Wish Upon a Teen helps make long-term hospital stays more comfortable for teenagers facing medical challenges. Vedder credits the "Metro Detroit grit" and unwavering support from his parents and local community for driving his competitive fire and work ethic. He has also been involved in creative projects, including a comic book series documenting his life story, aimed at inspiring young athletes to pursue their dreams regardless of obstacles.

Olympic Experience
  • 2-time Olympian
    • Olympic Games Milan 2026, 11th (Snowboardcross - Men)
    • Olympic Games Beijing 2022, 6th (Snowboardcross - Men), 9th (Snowboard Cross Team - Mixed)
World Championships Experience
  • Most recent: 2025 – 7th (Snowboardcross - Men)
  • Years of participation: Snowboardcross - Men 2019, 2021, 2023, 2025; Snowboard Cross Team - Mixed 2019
  • Top finish: 5th – 2019 (Snowboardcross - Men)
